lines.en_lit: a gatehouse, a portico [akhsadra], and a balcony the verse states: House; just as a house is designated for residence exclusion of those that are not designated for residence

might that I include even a bathroom, and a tannery, and a bathhouse, and a for immersion. the verse states: House; just as a house that is designed to honor so too, all that are designed to honor excluding those that are not designed to honor.

might that I include even Temple Mount and chambers and courtyards. the verse states: House; just as a house that is non-sacred, so too any that is non-sacred excluding those that are sacred. conclusive refutation

Rav Shmuel bar Yehuda taught before Rava: Six gates are exempt from mezuza: storehouse for hay, cattle barn, and woodshed, and a storehouse, and Median gate, and an unroofed gate, a gate that is not ten high. said to him: You began with six and you concluded with seven.

said to him: Median gate is tanna’im, as it was taught a dome, Rabbi Meir obligates in mezuza, and the Rabbis exempt And they agree that if, at the foot there are ten obligated in mezuza Abaye said: everyone agrees ten high and at the foot there are not three it is nothing. Alternatively, at the foot there are three is not ten high, it is nothing,

They disagree only ten and at the foot there are three but no the width four is enough to carve out complete four Rabbi Meir holds one carves out to complete And the Rabbis hold: One does not carve out to complete
The Sages taught A synagogue, a woman’s house, a house partners obligated in mezuza. obvious; Lest you say “Your house,” and not her house; “Your house,” and not their house, teaches us
And say indeed so the verse states: “So that your days be numerous, as well as the days of your sons” these require life, and these, do not require life?
Rather, why do I Your house, in accordance with of Rava, as Rava said: the way that you enter And when a person lifts he lifts his right foot first.

It was taught another A synagogue, a house partners, and a woman’s house become impure with leprosy obvious; Lest you say “And the one whom the house is his will come” His and not hers; his and not theirs, it teaches us

And say indeed so the verse states: “In a house of the land of your possession” Rather, why do I His, one who dedicates his house to himself who refuses to lend his vessels and says that he does not have The Holy One, Blessed be He, publicizes his when empty his house This excludes who lends his vessels to others;

And synagogue, does it become impure with leprosy? But wasn’t it taught might that synagogues and study halls become impure with leprosy. the verse states: And the one whom the house is his will come; that is designated for him, excluding those that are not designated for him

not difficult, This Rabbi Meir; that the Rabbis, as it was taught A synagogue in which there is a residence for the synagogue attendant is obligated in mezuza, a synagogue in which there is no residence, Rabbi Meir obligates and the Rabbis exempt
And if you wish, say this and that the Rabbis, and not difficult. This in which there is a place of residence; that in which there is not a place of residence.
And if you wish, say this and that in which there is not a place of residence,
